tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function is mildly impaired (elevated creatinine), and metabolic acidosis is suggested (low CO₂). Urinalysis shows significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Low HbA1c with glucosuria may indicate recent glycemic changes or renal glycosuria. Findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney disease with nephrotic-range proteinuria and metabolic derangement; further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
